fit_guidance.fitted: Runs close to body. If between sizes, we recommend sizing up for comfort.
fit_guidance.relaxed: Generous cut with ease through the body. True to size for most customers.
fit_guidance.sleeveless: Armhole cut may vary by vendor. Aurelia Couture tends to run slightly narrow in the shoulders.
fit_guidance.long-sleeve: Sleeve length is based on standard arm measurement. Petite customers may want to have sleeves hemmed.
fit_guidance.off-shoulder: Designed to sit below the collarbone. Ensure bust measurement matches — the neckline cannot be adjusted easily.
fit_guidance.one-shoulder: Asymmetric construction. Fit is most dependent on bust and torso length.
fit_guidance.cape-sleeve: Flowing sleeve requires no arm measurement. Focus on bust and waist fit.
fit_guidance.halter: Halter necklines work best for narrower shoulders. Size by bust measurement.

vendor_sizing_notes.Aurelia Couture: Runs slightly small, especially in the bust and shoulders. Consider sizing up one size.
vendor_sizing_notes.Nocturne: True to size across all styles. Consistent fit between collections.
vendor_sizing_notes.Bella Rosa: True to size for relaxed styles. Fitted styles may run snug at the waist — check waist measurement.
vendor_sizing_notes.Wanderlust: Generous, relaxed fit. Some customers find they can size down in casual styles.
vendor_sizing_notes.Glitz & Glam: Bodycon and fitted styles run very true. Sequin and embellished fabrics have less stretch — size up if unsure.
vendor_sizing_notes.Moonlit: Consistent sizing. Modest styles have extra ease built in for coverage.

general_tips: If you are between two sizes, consider the fit type: size up for 'fitted', stay true for 'relaxed'.
Stretch fabrics (jersey, knit) are more forgiving than structured fabrics (taffeta, organza, satin).
For evening gowns, we recommend sizing by bust measurement first, then checking waist.
All measurements are taken with the garment laid flat. Double the bust/waist/hip numbers for circumference.
